title: Tiss'shar
content:
{{Top|fa|canon=Tiss-shar}}
{{Species
|image=[[File:UulRhaShanHS.jpg]]
|name=Tiss'shar
|designation=[[Sentience/Legends|Sentient]]<ref name="Galaxy Guide 12: Aliens - Enemies and Allies">''[[Galaxy Guide 12: Aliens — Enemies and Allies]]''</ref>
|class=*[[Reptile/Legends|Reptile]]<ref name="Galaxy Guide 12: Aliens - Enemies and Allies" />

|races=
|height=1.5–1.8 [[Meter/Legends|meters]],<ref name="Galaxy Guide 12: Aliens - Enemies and Allies" /> averaging 1.65 meters<ref name="Ultimate Alien Anthology" />
|length=

|language=[[Tiss'shar (language)|Tiss'shar]]<ref name="Ultimate Alien Anthology" />
}}
{{Quote|As with most species, there exists no stereotypical manner that defines the Tiss'shar, though they are generally recognized as some of the best assassin and gunbeings credits can buy.|Tem Eliss, ''The University of Sanbra Guide to Intelligent Life''|<ref name="Galaxy Guide 12: Aliens - Enemies and Allies" />}}
The '''Tiss'shar''' were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of predatory [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[therapod]] [[Reptile/Legends|reptiles]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tiss'sharl]]. They were comparatively strong, dexterous, and intelligent, possessing slender frames with long necks and short tails. Their [[Mouth/Legends|mouths]] were filled with sharp [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]], and their [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] were dark and glassy.

Members of the species were well known for their considerable business savvy, and Tiss'shar businessbeings could be found throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. They were also known for their cunning [[Assassin/Legends|assassins]], who were sought after for their efficiency and business-like demeanor. Tiss'shar [[Politics/Legends|politics]] were dominated by the [[Tiss'sharl League]], a group of successful business leaders who ran Tiss'sharl's government. Tiss'shar politicians were known for their cutthroat practices, sometimes involving assassination of other members.

The species was caught up in an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] scheme eight months after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. Ordered by [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] to reduce the prices of one of their world's products, the President of the Tiss'sharl League was approached by an agent of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], only to discover that drawing out Rebels was the actual purpose behind Vader's demand.

==Biology and appearance==
{{Quote|They possess great strength and excellent agility, showing their hunter/predator heritage.|Tem Eliss, ''The University of Sanbra Guide to Intelligent Life''|<ref name="Galaxy Guide 12: Aliens - Enemies and Allies" />}}
[[File:Tissshar.jpg|thumb|left|upright|Two Tiss'shar, ''kal-ar'' (foreground) and ''sil-ar'' (background) at work.]]
The Tiss'shar were bipedal [[therapod]]s descended from predatory [[Reptile/Legends|reptilians]] native to the humid jungle continents of [[Tiss'sharl]].<ref name="Ultimate Alien Anthology" /> They were relatively strong and agile, with long necks, slender torsos, wider hips, and a short but wiry [[Limb/Legends|tail]]. Their [[Mouth/Legends|mouths]] were filled with short, sharp [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]], and their large tongues were pink and constantly moving. Their large [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] were [[Color/Legends|black]] and appeared glassy as a result of the clear, resilient film that covered and protected them.<ref name="Galaxy Guide 12: Aliens - Enemies and Allies" /> Many Tiss'shar had prominent sickle-shaped claws on the first toe of their feet; some also possessed a crest of feathers atop their heads.<ref name="Star Wars: Empire 31: The Price of Power">[[Empire 31|''Empire'' 31]]</ref>

[[File:Orlar-UAA.jpg|thumb|right|upright|An ''orl-ar'' Tiss'shar.]]
There were six [[Subspecies/Legends|subspecies]] of Tiss'shar, distinguished most easily by the markings on their scales.<ref name="Ultimate Alien Anthology" /> The ''[[sil-ar]]'' were the most common, and had diamond shaped markings on the backs of their heads and torsos, and on their limbs. The ''[[kal-ar]]'' were another common subspecies, having thin, concentric bands beginning at the base of their jaws and going all the way to their feet.<ref name="Galaxy Guide 12: Aliens - Enemies and Allies" /> Other subspecies included the ''[[ask-ar]]'', which had red markings resembling masks on their faces; the ''[[orl-ar]]'', which had green scales punctuated by yellow-orange stripes; the ''[[nil-ar]]'', which had thin red bands around their eyes, wrists, ankles, and tails; and the ''[[isk-ar]]'', which were albino Tiss'shar with translucent white scales and no other visible markings.<ref name="Ultimate Alien Anthology" />

==Society and culture==
{{Quote|I am also here to instruct each of you—league councilors, officers, the honorable Vice President—that your President is to suffer no repercussions for the Empire's decision. I ''do'' understand your local politics.|Darth Vader|<ref name="Star Wars: Empire 31: The Price of Power" />}}
Tiss'shar social structure was inextricably tied to business. Nearly all Tiss'shar families had a corporation as a patron, which provided the family with its material needs in exchange for the dedicated labor of its members.<ref name="Galaxy Guide 12: Aliens - Enemies and Allies" /> Patron corporations regularly supplied food, housing, health care, education, and security services.<ref name="Ultimate Alien Anthology" /> While most companies native to Tiss'sharl focused on enviro-technology and modern weaponry,<ref name="Galaxy Guide 12: Aliens - Enemies and Allies" /> they also produced many other high-end products such as [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrives]] and [[Droid/Legends|droids]].<ref name="Ultimate Alien Anthology" /> Tiss'sharl was open to companies from offworld, and some of the most prominent corporations from around [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] set up offices there; however, these companies' less generous benefits packages often meant they could only attract less skilled Tiss'shar to their payroll.<ref name="Galaxy Guide 12: Aliens - Enemies and Allies" />

The predatory instincts of the Tiss'shar, redirected into the realm of business, served them well. While no stereotype fully defined the species, they often were considered aggressive, resourceful, calculating businessbeings. These traits also made them excellent [[Assassin/Legends|assassins]] and gunfighters, renowned galaxywide for their efficiency. Most Tiss'shar had an appreciation for the art of the deal, and conducted their business legitimately; even those who chose the path of the assassin remembered that they were conducting business and respected their contracts.<ref name="Galaxy Guide 12: Aliens - Enemies and Allies" /> Tiss'shar tended to focus solely on success, tackling problems with a detached and calculating approach. They often preferred subterfuge over overt physical approaches when solving disputes.<ref name="Ultimate Alien Anthology" />

[[File:Tisssharl_League.jpg|thumb|left|350px|The Tiss'sharl League meets with Lord Vader.]]
Government among the Tiss'shar was also tied to business. Tiss'sharl was governed by the [[Tiss'sharl League]], a body consisting of those businessbeings whose companies posted the highest profits for the previous five local years, with each local year being roughly eleven standard months long.<ref name="Galaxy Guide 12: Aliens - Enemies and Allies" /> The body was not restricted to Tiss'shar; foreigners were also eligible to become a part of the League,<ref name="Ultimate Alien Anthology" /> which was headed by a President and [[Vice President/Legends|Vice President]], with officers and league councilors filling out other roles. Tiss'shar [[Politics/Legends|politics]] by the time of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] were extremely cutthroat, with advancement often occurring as a result of assassination.<ref name="Star Wars: Empire 31: The Price of Power" />

The [[Tiss'shar (language)|Tiss'shar]] was made up of hisses, shrieks, and lisping vocalizations. The written form of Tiss'shar consisted of sharp lines and curves in series, and was described as possessing a violent-looking aesthetic.<ref name="Ultimate Alien Anthology" />

Tiss'shar typically did not wear clothing other than a simple tool harness,<ref name="Preparing for War">{{WEGCite|story=Preparing for War|book=The Far Orbit Project}}</ref> though that was far from an absolute rule. Citizens of the capital [[City/Legends|city]] could be seen wearing garments of various sorts covering their upper torsos. Members of the Tiss'sharl League and their guards often wore elaborate outfits about their necks and shoulders, and guards also wore wide-brimmed helmets. Assassins sometimes wore a face-concealing cowl.<ref name="Star Wars: Empire 31: The Price of Power" />

Tiss'shar cuisine, befitting their predatory nature, was served unheated. Meat was served still attached to the bone of the creature of origin, with no attempt made to disguise the source; small animals were served whole.<ref name="Star Wars: Empire 31: The Price of Power" />

==History==
{{Quote|How can the Rebellion protect Tiss'sharl from the shadows?|President Si-Di-Ri|<ref name="Star Wars: Empire 31: The Price of Power" />}}
Tiss'sharl was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Xappyh sector/Legends|Xappyh sector]], located in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="eao">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> Sometime prior to [[3976 BBY]], [[Mandalore the Ultimate]] ordered the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]] to expand outward. Among the peoples they discovered were the Tiss'shar, many of whom became recruits among the Mandalorians, who had begun to accept members of non-[[Taung/Legends|Taung]] races.<ref name="EGW">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref>

In [[3704 BBY]], the [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] explorer [[Freia Kallea/Legends|Freia Kallea]] connected the nearby [[Morellian Trail]] to the [[Celanon Spur/Legends|Spurs of Celanon]] as part of the super-[[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]] that came to be known as the [[Hydian Way/Legends|Hydian Way]]. This opened up this region to the rest of the galaxy, and the space that included Tiss'sharl was explored between [[3000 BBY|3000]] and [[1000 BBY/Legends|1000 BBY]].<ref name="The Essential Atlas">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> The Tiss'shar came under the undisputed authority of the Tiss'sharl League in approximately 1000 BBY; the rule of the League provided stability and abundance for the Tiss'shar, though it also eventually fostered the cutthroat politics that characterized it during the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Imperial Era/Legends|era]].<ref name="Star Wars: Empire 31: The Price of Power" /> During the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], Tiss'sharl fell within territory controlled by the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]].<ref name="The Essential Atlas" />

[[File:TisssharEmblem-NEGWT.svg|thumb|right|Symbol of the Tiss'shar]]
By the time of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the Tiss'shar were well-integrated into the galaxy. They were key players in the galactic economy, with Tiss'sharl the focus of extensive import and export activity, often generating a trade surplus. Many galactic corporations, including [[Tagge Company/Legends|TaggeCo]], had set up offices on the planet.<ref name="Galaxy Guide 12: Aliens - Enemies and Allies" /> The world was loyal to the Empire, with [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Giiedt]] serving as Imperial liaison to Tiss'sharl.<ref name="Star Wars: Empire 31: The Price of Power" />

The election prior to the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] left [[Si-Di-Ri]] in the Presidency of the Tiss'sharl League, with his friend [[Astre-De-Kay]] as Vice President, and [[Geor-Dan-Thi]] appointed to the League Council. Geor-Dan-Thi soon rose in power, as [[Mayor/Legends|Mayor]] [[Antha-Kres]], [[Secretary/Legends|Secretary]] [[Lai-O-Sid]], and Astre-De-Kay all soon died under questionable circumstances. Many, including Si-Di-Ri himself, believed Geor-Dan-Thi, by then Vice President, was not finished rising through the League's hierarchy through assassination.<ref name="Star Wars: Empire 31: The Price of Power" />

[[File:SiDiRi.jpg|thumb|upright|left|President Si-Di-Ri]]
The Galactic Civil War brought new challenges. [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] visited the world three times in a year, each time demanding a lower selling price of TaggeCo [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] [[Blaster power pack/Legends|cartridges]]. Vader's third visit occurred eight months after the Battle of Yavin, following the [[Death/Legends|death]] of Moff Giiedt. Vader brought Giiedt's replacement, [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Demmings]], and spared him the task of demanding yet another price cut, to 1,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] per 5,000-cartridge container.<ref name="Star Wars: Empire 31: The Price of Power" />

Si-Di-Ri secretly met with former [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Timi Rotramel]] of [[Dac/Legends|Mon Calamari]], who was sent by the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] to offer Tiss'sharl protection from the Empire, including the establishment of an Alliance base. Despite the pressure Imperial demands were making on Tiss'sharl's economy, Si-Di-Ri refused Rotramel's offer, as the Rebellion was on the run and in hiding from the Empire, and thus was not a viable alternative in his eyes. His refusal occurred just as Vader arrived at the meeting. The raise in prices had been part of a ploy by Vader to draw a Rebel emissary to Tiss'sharl, though he had expected [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] instead of Rotramel. Vader killed the Senator, and, in light of the President's loyalty and unwitting assistance in the gambit, allowed TaggeCo to retain its current prices. Si-Di-Ri, realizing he was ultimately a pawn in spite of his position, and realizing the position was not worth the constant risk of assassination, chose to resign his post, the first President of the Tiss'sharl League in recent memory to do so.<ref name="Star Wars: Empire 31: The Price of Power" />

==Tiss'shar in the galaxy==
{{Quote|Tiss'shar encountered off of their homeworld are often of two disciplines: business or killing.|Tem Eliss, ''The University of Sanbra Guide to Intelligent Life''|<ref name="Galaxy Guide 12: Aliens - Enemies and Allies" />}}
[[File:Uul-Rha-Shan.jpg|thumb|right|upright|Uul-Rha-Shan, Tiss'shar assassin and bodyguard]]
The Tiss'shar were known across the galaxy as shrewd, resourceful businessbeings. Tiss'shar merchants could be found on all of the major hyperlanes, and Tiss'shar corporations were aggressive in their search for new markets and business opportunities throughout the galaxy. Tiss'shar who didn't care for more legitimate means of gathering wealth often became corporate or freelance killers.<ref name="Ultimate Alien Anthology" /> such as the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Xufal D'uat]].<ref name="Stock Ships">''[[Stock Ships]]''</ref>

A large number of Tiss'shar found their way into the [[Corporate Sector/Legends|Corporate Sector]].<ref name="Galaxy Guide 12: Aliens - Enemies and Allies" /> Eleven percent of the population of [[Bonadan/Legends|Bonadan]], one of the Sector's busiest ports, was Tiss'shar, and a significant population of them lived on the urban planet [[Media (planet)|Media]] on the Sector's [[Lucaya Cross]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]].<ref name="The Essential Atlas" /> Some Tiss'shar in the sector found work in legitimate business, while others served as "protection agents" and corporate assassins.<ref name="Galaxy Guide 12: Aliens - Enemies and Allies" /> [[Uul-Rha-Shan]], a former gunrunner turned gunslinger, was hired by [[Corporate Sector Authority/Legends|Corporate Sector Authority]] [[Viceprex/Legends|Viceprex]] [[Mirkovig Hirken]] to serve as his [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguard]].<ref name="Han Solo and the Corporate Sector Sourcebook">''[[Han Solo and the Corporate Sector Sourcebook]]''</ref>

Yet other Tiss'shar turned to [[Pirate/Legends|piracy]]. One of the most infamous was [[Abin-Ral-Xufush]], the notorious leader of the [[Shadow Wing]] pirate [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]].<ref name="Pirates & Privateers">''[[Pirates & Privateers]]''</ref> Another Tiss'shar pirate was [[Pao-Neh-Lo]], an experienced and merciless member of the crew of the [[Privateer/Legends|privateering]] [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|vessel]] ''[[Far Orbit]]''.<ref name="Preparing for War" />

The Tiss'shar [[Tra-Skan-Lor]] served as the [[Director/Legends|Director]] of the [[Special Acquisitions Branch]] of the [[Library of the Republic]] and caused the ''[[Sayings]]'' of [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Uueg Tching]] of [[Atrisia/Legends|Atrisia]] to become deeply embeded within the culture of the Branch.<ref name="The Written Word">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/source/writtenword/index.html?page=1|text=The Written Word|int=The Written Word}}</ref>

The droid manufacturer [[Uhr-Vah-Vo TechWorks]] was operated by Tiss'shar, and was purchased by the [[InterGalactic Banking Clan/Legends|InterGalactic Banking Clan]] several years prior to the outbreak of the Clone Wars.<ref name="Scavenger's Guide to Droids">''[[Scavenger's Guide to Droids]]''</ref>

As [[Non-huMan|non-humans]], Tiss'shar faced some discrimination during the time of the Galactic Empire. Like many non-human races, they were not welcome on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].<ref name="Star Wars: Empire 31: The Price of Power" /> Non-Tiss'shar sometimes referred to them derogatorily as "snakes."<ref name="Star Wars: Empire 31: The Price of Power" /><ref name="Han Solo at Stars' End">''[[Han Solo at Stars' End]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|The Tiss'shar were kind of tough to get a handle on at first but now I'm really enjoying drawing them too.|[[Joe Corroney]]|<ref name="The Official Joe Corroney Discussion Thread">{{JCFCite|url=posts/18379083|board=Literature|thread=The Official Joe Corroney Discussion Thread|user=Joe_Corroney|date=2005-02-12|time=4:33 AM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20150912025138/http://boards.theforce.net/threads/the-official-joe-corroney-discussion-thread.15797039/page-4}}</ref>}}
The Tiss'shar first appeared in the form of the reptilian thug Uul-Rha-Shan in [[Brian Daley]]'s novel ''[[Han Solo at Stars' End]]'', in [[1979]],<ref name="Han Solo at Stars' End" /> though the species would go unnamed until Uul-Rha-Shan's write-up in the ''[[Han Solo and the Corporate Sector Sourcebook]]'', published in [[1993]].<ref name="Han Solo and the Corporate Sector Sourcebook" /> The species itself would first be explored in ''[[Galaxy Guide 12: Aliens — Enemies and Allies]]'' two years later.<ref name="Galaxy Guide 12: Aliens - Enemies and Allies" />

[[File:UulRhaShaan.jpg|thumb|left|Uul-Rha-Shan as he appeared in the ''Han Solo at Stars' End'' comic adaptation.]]
The appearance of the Tiss'shar has varied with time. In the [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] adaptation of [[Han Solo at Stars' End (comic strip)|''Han Solo at Stars' End'']], Uul-Rha-Shan appeared as a robust, almost amphibian alien with a protruding snout.<ref name="Han Solo at Stars' End Comic">[[Han Solo at Stars' End (comic strip)|''Han Solo at Stars' End'' comic]]</ref> This depiction was apparently forgotten years later, when [[Mike Vilardi]] illustrated a more slender, lizard-like depiction of him for ''Han Solo and the Corporate Sector Sourcebook''.<ref name="Han Solo and the Corporate Sector Sourcebook" /> This version of the Tiss'shar would inspire later images drawn throughout [[West End Games]]'s publications,<ref name="Alliance Intelligence Reports">''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]''</ref><ref name="Galaxy Guide 12: Aliens - Enemies and Allies" /><ref name="Pirates & Privateers" /> as well as the image in [[Wizards of the Coast]]'s ''[[Ultimate Alien Anthology]]''.<ref name="Ultimate Alien Anthology" /> In [[Empire 31|''Empire'' 31]], the Tiss'shar acquired an appearance much more like what dinosaurs such as [[Wikipedia:Velociraptor|''Velociraptor'']] were widely believed to look like at the time of the issue's illustration, complete with the characteristic sickle-shaped claw on the foot's second digit.<ref name="Drawing from the Past">{{SW|url=news/drawing-from-the-past-how-prehistoric-creatures-influence-star-wars-designs|text=Drawing from the Past: How Prehistoric Creatures Influence ''Star Wars'' Designs|int=Back from the Drawing Board|nolive=1}}</ref>

''Star Wars: Empire 31'' artist [[Joe Corroney]] at first had difficulty drawing the reptilian protagonists of the issue, but eventually adjusted and came to enjoy the task.<ref name="The Official Joe Corroney Discussion Thread" /> The depictions of various Tiss'shar in that comic suggest far more outward variety than the six subspecies described in the ''[[Ultimate Alien Anthology]]''.<ref name="Star Wars: Empire 31: The Price of Power" /><ref name="Ultimate Alien Anthology" />
